digest.component_or_area: Gerrit + GitHub Actions serial auto-lander project infrastructure
propositions: Parallel agent sessions race to land on the fast-forward-only main, forcing manual rebase-to-tip retries that burn CI runs and agent attention.
A serial auto-lander service rebases the front Autosubmit change/chain to tip, awaits a fresh Verified, and ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA with zero further agent attention.
Agents interact via a single project land command returning one typed terminal outcome (merged | needs_rebase | ci_failed | review_failed) so they never correlate Gerrit votes/labels themselves.
Landing/merge-queueing logic lives in project infra (infra/ + CLI) and must not add Gerrit-specific landing logic to src/rebar core, keeping rebar platform-agnostic.
Safety invariants are preserved: nothing lands untested (FFO + strict Verified), the two-vote gate is never weakened, and stacks are never partial-landed but handed back whole on failure.
Cross-stack batching (Phase 2) is deferred/rejected on this infra in favor of bounded speculation, promoted only on measured serial-treadmill pain.
